title: rhus canadensis var. trilobata
intro: Rhus canadensis var. trilobata, commonly known as skunkbush or fragrant sumac, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, colds, and skin conditions, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Rhus canadensis var. trilobata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its health benefits.
